items.title: Keep branches short-lived
items.avoid: Two-week feature branches that drift far from `main`.
items.prefer: Branches that live 1–3 days; sync with `main` every morning.
items.why: Conflict surface grows roughly linearly with branch lifetime.
items.example:
```bash
# Daily ritual on a private feature branch:
git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main
```

items.title: Turn on `diff3` style markers
items.avoid: Resolving conflicts blind, without seeing the common ancestor.
items.why: Seeing the ancestor reveals what each side changed *from* — usually the deciding context.

items.title: Run tests after resolving
items.avoid: Pushing a resolved merge without running anything.
items.prefer: `pytest` (or your test command) on the resolved tree before pushing.
items.why: Resolution can compile but silently break behaviour — tests catch semantic mismatches.
items.example:
```bash
# After resolving and `git add`:
pytest -q && git rebase --continue
```

items.title: Use specialized tooling for notebooks and binaries
items.avoid: Trying to read raw JSON conflict markers in `.ipynb` files.
items.prefer: `nbdime` for semantic notebook diffs; `nbstripout` to keep outputs out of Git; Git LFS for legitimate binaries.
items.why: Generic three-way merge is useless on binary or quasi-binary formats.

items.title: Abort early when a rebase becomes a swamp
items.avoid: Slogging through 20 conflicts that re-surface per commit.
items.prefer: `git rebase --abort`; merge `main` in instead (one conflict surface, one resolution).
items.why: Rebase replays each commit individually — same logical conflict can recur many times.
